sections.paragraph:
πονέω (πόνος) fut. πονέσω LXX; 1 aor. ἐπονεσα LXX, inf. πονέσαι (Just., D. 90, 2) and πονῆσαι (TestSol 6:13 D); pf. ptc. pl. gen. πεπονηκότων (Ath., R. 17 p. 69, 11). Pass.: aor. ptc. neut. pl. πονηθέντα (Just., A II, 10, 2) (Pind. et al. [the mid. as dep. as early as Hom.]; ins, pap, LXX; PsSol 2:14; TestSol; JosAs 25:3; Jos., Ant. 12, 240; 15, 33; Just.; Ath., R. 17 p. 69, 11) to engage in any kind of hard work, toil, undergo trouble ἐπί τινι about or in behalf of someone ἐπὶ καταπονουμένῳ (s. καταπονέω) B 20:2; D 5:2. πονεῖ ἡ ψυχή μου ἐπὶ τοῖς υἱοῖς τῶν ἀνθρώπων Ox 1 verso, 17f (GTh 28) (of the ψυχή as Philo, Somn. 1, 255). An uncertain restoration εἰ πο̣ν̣ε̣[ῖ]ς AcPl Ha 7, 30.—DELG s.v. πένομαι.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pl. = plural

gen. gen. = genitive

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

dep. dep. = deponent

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

GTh GTh = Gospel of Thomas in Coptic or in Oxy. documents—Lists 1

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 850
